reviews.review_description: If your game had a skip button or a start over button that would be great but it doesn’t and I can start over I just start from the point where I end up at and it’s annoying because there is not a button to start over or skip button so please make one

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for playing my game and for your feedback! :) Skipping is not an option since it's important for the story to make it through. I'll implement a reset-button, but it may take it little. :)

reviews.review_description: I have played quite a bit of climbing games in VR. I didn't have high hopes but I was pleasantly surprised by how engaging the environment is. I just wish I could mute the music to play my own. Also I couldn't figure out how to pause the game which was a bit annoying especially with the timer going down.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing and rating my game! :) I put your points on my list! Cheers, Can

reviews.review_title: A great alternative to fps games
reviews.review_description:
I love this game. I like shooters,but this type of 🎯 
is refreshing. The only glitch I found was the tendency to fall through the window cleaners elevator. Rest of the game is great. Id be happy for the developer to make more games like this. It's great value. Give it a go.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing, glad you liked it so much! And thanks for letting me know the glitch, I´ll check that! :)

reviews.review_title: 🧗 The Ladder – The Only Way Out Is Up
reviews.review_description:
The Ladder is an intriguing and remarkably well-executed game that drops you into a dystopian world where you gradually piece together the story by climbing ever higher through complex, interconnected levels. It’s a clever mix of puzzle-solving and climbing mechanics that keeps you constantly engaged.

The grip system feels spot-on — every fall is entirely on you — and the higher you climb, the more you realize just how far you’ve come from the ground below. The timer on your wrist adds an extra layer of mystery and tension (I’m still not sure what happens when it runs out, but I’m not eager to find out!).

With a compelling narrative, immersive graphics, and satisfying mechanics, The Ladder delivers both challenge and atmosphere in equal measure. A must-play for anyone who loves climbing adventures with a psychological twist.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing! :)

reviews.review_description:
Great graphics, love the atmosphere and very different levels that keep you climbing up to see more. Well crafted maps

----

The environments look great, each level looks different to the last and get more challenging. I'm currently stuck on level 4 but I can already tell this game is worth trying out if you like climbing

The climbing is simple - there's no stamina - and that's a good thing. Some of the things you grab move, but the game won't auto rotate you so it's very comfortable you shouldn't need strong VR legs

Atmosphere, graphics, audio, lighting - all great, much better than I was expecting from a solo dev! It's a bit annoying that you have to wait for the dialog to end before you can start climbing and i wish the audio had subtitles

Sadly it doesn't save your progress - while I imagine this is a short less than an hour experience i haven't got to the end and I've done 3 sessions but people keep interrupting me and I have to start again. (Also there's a 1 hour timer on your hand). 

I wish there was an option to turn off the main characters groans while climbing. Its not too bad but i thibk people might want that as an option. Also every 5 minutes your timer tells you to keep climbing, i quite like that, creates a sense of urgency

I think on the 4th level I'm stuck because I fell down and I can't seem to get back up the buttons don't work. Of course if i didn't fall it's not a problem.... I really should have died falling that far anyway...

I want to keep playing, that's a good sign, and assuming the experience is short i think it's a good thing because climbing is tiring on your arms and I'm glad the dev has made a refined polished experience that keeps you wanting to progress to see what's above rather than a repetitive boring long a** climbing game that takes hours to finish.

I recommend it!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your nice comment! I just uploaded an update and now sounds are less annoying, the game saves automatically und bugs are gone now! Cheers Can

reviews.review_description:
Updated Review:

Thank you for updating the audio!  It's a lot nicer and now I'm noticing how detailed the ambient sounds are, like the subtle hiss of air vents as you climb past them.

The atmosphere really is wonderful. I realize you're a lone developer and likely don't have the time or the resources to create a big adventure game set in this world, so I can only hope some wise publisher sees this, hires you, then gives you whatever resources you need. Looking around at the first checkpoint I really wanted to wander off and explore. 😁

I never imagined I'd be this impressed with a $5 indie game!  The art direction is better than many big budget games and the climbing is challenging and very fun.

I do have a short wishlist:

I'm scared of heights, but I love being terrified in VR so it'd be nice if you could fall to your death. I was scared to fall until I accidentally did, then when nothing happened I was a bit disappointed. Maybe just a fade to black with a yell that's suddenly cut short?

Your hands often aren't aligned with the rungs you're gripping.

And finally, it'd be nice if you could tap your wrist to bring up a menu to let you quit or restart, but this isn't really important. If you did add a menu and falling to your death, maybe the menu could have an option to disable that for people who don't like to terrify themselves in VR? 🙂


First impressions:

Great art direction, fun gameplay, absolutely terrible audio!

While some textures are extremely blurry the overall look of the game is excellent. Top notch art direction.

I've only ascended one level but the "puzzle climbing" gameplay is really fun so far.

Unfortunately the audio is so awful it makes me not want to play it.  I'm going to put it aside and hope that the developers update the game.

Your character lets out a constant stream of noise, "Uh" "Oh" "Ah" "Ugh", as you climb. You sound like the most out of shape, whiny climber ever to grab the rung of a ladder. It's maddening and there's no way to turn it off. If every once in a while you let out a groan or grunt that would be fine.

Grabbing anything makes a sharp metallic "ping" like your hands are metallic.

So it's "Uh ping Oh ping Ugh ping" all the way to the top.

Please, please work on the audio! There's a great game buried under all that cacophony. :)

reviews.review_title: Worth your time if you like climbing
reviews.review_description: I enjoy a good climbing game and for $5, and a solo dev, this game hit the spot. A fun sci-fi theme, they took a shot at adding a story, which was nice. I thought the time to grip was just ever so slightly off, like a fraction of a second, but once you accounted for that you were good and just took your time. I finished it in one session and had a good time and would recommend it. Nit picks - maybe have the light next to the buttons you press change from red to green. I don't need the arm band telling me the time or to rush every 10 minutes. Maybe instead of saying what it does, it tell you the time left. For $5 though, it's tough to complain, I came for the climbing and was satisfied with the new take on the scenery and light puzzling.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot :) Glad you had a good time!

reviews.review_title: Climb your way to your girlfriend.
reviews.review_description:
Climb your way to the top through mysteries locations.  You have 1hour to achieve your goal. Don’t know what will happen if you fail that time. I finished it in time 😜.

There are 5 different locations that you have to conquer . 

When the game starts, you hear your girlfriend talking. And you have to ‘climb’  to her. After finishing, I understand more about the story. I was hanging between life and dead. Unfortunately I finished the game ‘dead’. With a crying girlfriend as results.

Graphics are very good.
Love it.

Check my gameplay: 
https://youtu.be/wKx3ohzZHd4?si=EynwK_fq_hnun3Hk

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing and rating my game! :)

reviews.review_description: Fun climbing game with some fairly easy puzzle elements and a Story with an end i wasn't expecting. For the price its well worth it definitely recommend picking up if you like climbing and have 40min or so to kill.

developer_response.body: Thank you much for playing, really appreciate it! Cheers, Can
